metadata.title: Thermostat Control Loop
metadata.thesis: A thermostat controls room temperature by comparing the current temperature to a target set point, turning the heater on when the room is too cold, and shutting it off once the target is reached.
metadata.core_thesis: A thermostat controls room temperature by comparing the current temperature to a target set point, turning the heater on when the room is too cold, and shutting it off once the target is reached.
governing_assumptions: A thermostat does not directly create heat; it controls whether the heating system runs.
The room temperature changes over time, so the system must repeatedly compare the current state to the desired state.

backbone.principle: Feedback Control: A control system measures the current state, compares it to a target, triggers an action when there is a gap, and stops the action once the gap closes.

clusters.label: Sensing & Triggering
clusters.description: The thermostat first detects the room temperature and decides whether the heating system should be activated.

subnodes.label: Temperature Comparison
subnodes.mechanism: The thermostat continuously compares the current room temperature to the user-selected set point. If the room is below the target, the system detects a temperature gap that needs correction.

subnodes.label: Call For Heat
subnodes.mechanism: When the thermostat detects that the room is colder than the set point, it closes the control circuit and sends a signal that tells the furnace or heater to start running.

clusters.label: Heating & Shutoff
clusters.description: Once the heating system is on, warm air raises the room temperature until the thermostat detects that the target has been reached.

subnodes.label: Heat Raises Room Temperature
subnodes.mechanism: The furnace or heater adds thermal energy to the room, which gradually raises the air temperature and reduces the gap between the current temperature and the set point.

subnodes.label: Feedback Shutoff
subnodes.mechanism: As the room warms, the thermostat keeps measuring the temperature. Once the room reaches the target set point, the thermostat opens the circuit, which stops the heating system and prevents overheating.

learning_prerequisites.rationale: The system must first detect a temperature gap and activate the heater before heating and shutoff behavior can make sense.

frameworks.name: Closed-Loop Control
frameworks.statement: Measure the current state, compare it to a target, act on the difference, and stop acting when the difference disappears.
